response.justification: Wei Chen is a web and full-stack software developer with roughly 17 years of experience spanning TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and mobile development. While technically competent and prolific, the candidate has no discernible background in SoC or IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI/deep-learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD systems — the core competencies this Principal Performance Architect role demands. The most hardware-adjacent work on record is low-latency microservice development and an academic wave-field synthesis project, neither of which approaches the chip-level performance analysis required. The educational background (two Bachelor's degrees, no advanced degree) and the entirely software-product-oriented career trajectory place this candidate far outside the target profile.
strengths: 17+ years of continuous engineering experience demonstrating sustained technical growth and adaptability across diverse domains.
Proven low-latency and high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, DLang) indicates some performance-engineering sensibility.
Recent AI-adjacent work at re:cinq on MCP code generators and agentic infrastructure shows awareness of the AI tooling space.
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or any chip-level performance analysis — the primary technical requirement of the role.
No background in deep learning, neural network training or inference, GPU workloads, or SIMD/MIMD architectures (required and preferred qualifications both unmet).

Educational credentials (Bachelor's only, in CS and Fine Arts) and career trajectory are entirely in software product and web development, with no signal of a pivot toward hardware or systems architecture.

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No AI/ML or deep-learning workload expertise
